EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and cold front brought heavy snow to the Sierra Nevada mountains, and isolated high winds to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep moisture tap to the south of strong Pacific Northwest low pressure brought heavy rain and strong, gusty winds to western Nevada on the 24th and 25th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Typically windier areas (Washoe Valley, Galena, Five Mile Flat, 5WSW Gardnerville) had high wind gusts since 0600PST on the 24th but more widespread coverage in populated areas did not occur until 1000PST. Here is a list of the highest wind gusts: Galena Creek Bridge 102 mph at 1010PST, Washoe Valley 79 mph at 0610PST with a report of an overturned tractor trailer, Five Mile Flat with 60 mph at 0526PST, and 4SSW of Reno with 61 mph at 1022PST.  In addition to valley/foothills high winds, Virginia Peak and Slide Mountain gusted to between 110 to 130 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep moisture tap to the south of strong Pacific Northwest low pressure brought heavy rain and strong, gusty winds to western Nevada on the 24th and 25th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Here is a list of noted high wind gusts: Sweetwater NDOT with 78 mph at 1111PST, Walker Lake NDOT with 72 mph at 1505PST, and Schurz NDOT with 58 mph at 1414PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching storm system brought localized southwesterly downslope winds off the Spring Mountains into the west side of the Las Vegas Valley. The winds produced isolated damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low pressure dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into the northern Sierra between the 18th and the 21st followed by another low on the 23rd. This brought gusty winds and periods of snow to western Nevada, with periods of heavy snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread gusts of 35 to 45 mph occurred on the 18th and 19th ahead of a major winter storm.  Local high wind gusts occurred in wind prone areas, including: 62 at the Washoe Valley NDOT site at 1300PST on the 18th, Galena RAWS with 72 mph at 0300PST on the 19th, and 61 mph at 0600PST on the 18th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low pressure dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into the northern Sierra between the 18th and the 21st followed by another low on the 23rd. This brought gusty winds and periods of snow to western Nevada, with periods of heavy snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This slow moving storm brought an average of 18 to 30 inches of snowfall in two periods, one from the evening of the 19th to the morning of the 20th and another period about 24 hours later.  In addition to snowfall, gusty winds occurred on the 18th and 19th ahead of the main snowfall with strong ridge winds continuing into the 20th. Local high wind gusts occurred, with 66 mph at Little Valley at 1200PST on the 18th. Meanwhile, Slide Mountain (9650 ft) gusted to 100 mph at 1440PST on the 18th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low pressure dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into the northern Sierra between the 18th and the 21st followed by another low on the 23rd. This brought gusty winds and periods of snow to western Nevada, with periods of heavy snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread gusts of 35 to 45 mph occurred early on the 23rd. Brief and isolated high wind gusts occurred at the Walker Lake RAWS between 0300PST and 0424PST, with a 67 mph gust at 0424PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low pressure dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into the northern Sierra between the 18th and the 21st followed by another low on the 23rd. This brought gusty winds and periods of snow to western Nevada, with periods of heavy snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A widespread 10 to 16 inches of snow fell in 12 to 14 hours with this system. Gusty winds after the main snowfall caused blowing of snow which made some measurements difficult. Here are some specific snowfall totals: Mt. Rose with an estimated 20 inches, Daggett Pass with 16 inches, Incline Village with 15 inches, and Glenbrook with 10 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold low pressure dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into the northern Sierra between the 18th and the 21st followed by another low on the 23rd. This brought gusty winds and periods of snow to western Nevada, with periods of heavy snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph occurred, especially from late evening on the 22nd through early morning on the 23rd. Isolated high wind gusts occurred in wind prone areas including: Galena RAWS with 65 mph, Five Mile Flat NDOT and the National Weather Service office with 66 mph, and Washoe Valley NDOT with 62 mph. Two power outages due to downed power lines were reported in the Sparks Tribune.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms fueled by a tropical moisture tap pounded the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days.  Extremely heavy snow and widespread flooding resulted.

Countless trees were knocked down, along with several power lines and poles. Some residents were without power for three days or more.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm produced areas of high winds over the Owens Valley and Mojave Desert, and locally heavy snow in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong downslope wind event affected the eastern Sierra slopes, the Owens Valley, and Esmeralda County, Nevada. A driver on Highway 95 approximately 12 miles south of Tonopah estimated a gust of 80 mph at 1800 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm produced areas of high winds over the Owens Valley and Mojave Desert, and locally heavy snow in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon during a downslope wind event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first of two significant systems within 3 days moved through the region on the 18th and brought moderate amounts of snowfall and gusty winds.

Lesser amounts fell further south, with only 3 inches for Glenbrook and Daggett Pass.||In addition to heavy snowfall, winds gusted between 35-50 mph for several sites in the Carson Range.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system driving into the Pacific Northwest dragged a cold front through northeast California and western Nevada on the 14th and 15th. This brought gusty winds along with a moderate Sierra snowfall and a light snowfall for some lower elevations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Most wind gusts were in the 35 to 50 mph range on the 14th. However, wind prone areas had occasional gusts over 70 mph, including 72 mph at the Sweetwater NDOT at 1000PST and 79 mph at the Walker NDOT at 1844PST. The localized strong winds were sufficient to blow over an empty tractor trailer truck (cross wind) on highway 95 five miles north of Hawthorne, causing two injuries.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts were generally between 35 and 45 mph with this system. However,  especially wind prone areas saw gusts over 65 mph, including Galena and Washoe Valley RAWS with 74 mph and 68 mph, respectively, and the National Weather Service in Reno with 66 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system driving into the Pacific Northwest dragged a cold front through northeast California and western Nevada on the 14th and 15th. This brought gusty winds along with a moderate Sierra snowfall and a light snowfall for some lower elevations.

Local amounts ranged as high as 12 inches north and west of Reno and Carson City. Snow caused nearly 100 accidents, with many during the morning of the 17th. Also, weather caused 4 flight cancellations and 13 delays at the Reno-Tahoe airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm system brought high winds to northern and central Nevada on March 30th.  Winds gusts over 60 mph were common across the area with a few gusts over 70 mph.  This resulted in some structural damage and a few trees blown down in Elko.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm system brought high winds to northern and central Nevada on March 30th.  Winds gusts over 60 mph were common across the area with a few gusts over 70 mph.  This resulted in some structural damage and a few trees blown down in Elko.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm system brought high winds to northern and central Nevada on March 30th.  Winds gusts over 60 mph were common across the area with a few gusts over 70 mph.  This resulted in some structural damage and a few trees blown down in Elko.

Several reports were received of some shingles being blown off of roofs and one house had some siding blown off. Four momentary power outages affected about half of Elko due to the high winds causing power lines to come together. At about 11:48 am two power lines coming together caused sparks that fell to the ground and ignited a brush fire that burned about half an acre off of east Idaho Street.

Here are some specific wind gusts: 72 mph at the Galena RAWS at 1700PST, 66 mph at the Five Mile Flat RAWS at 1600PST, 64 mph at the Wolf Run Golf Course in SW Reno at 1640PST, and 60 mph at the National Weather Service.  In Carson City, strong winds caused difficulty controlling a fire which caused extensive damage to a furniture store (250 to 500k according to fire chief) and a hair salon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds were sustained at 40 to 50 mph on Lake Tahoe from 0200PST to 0500PST. Slide Mountain recorded a gust to 132 mph. Other high wind gusts include 61 mph at the Little Valley RAWS at 0238PST and 95 mph at the Galena RAWS at 0303PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm slammed into the region on the 27th and 28th bringing extensive wind damage to the Reno-Carson City area along with a moderate snowfall to the Carson Range east of Lake Tahoe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust was recorded in southwest Reno near the Arrowcreek Golf Course at 0400PST. The Reno area saw widespread gusts between 60 and 80 mph. Other specific high wind gusts include 107 mph at the Galena Creek Bridge at 0525PST, 81 mph at the Wolf Run Golf Course in southwest Reno, and 70 mph at the National Weather Service Office at 0708PST. ||The exceptionally high winds caused widespread damage, with a Nevada Energy estimate of 5000 homes and businesses without power in the Reno area. Other damage included two trailers blown over on highway 395 in Reno, numerous fences and hundreds of trees uprooted (including a 70 ft pine), as well as many power lines downed. In addition to damage, more than a dozen flights into the Reno-Tahoe airport were canceled due to high winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm slammed into the region on the 27th and 28th bringing extensive wind damage to the Reno-Carson City area along with a moderate snowfall to the Carson Range east of Lake Tahoe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Hawthorne airport AWOS recorded this gust at 1200PST along with sustained winds of 40 to 50 mph from 1100PST to 1200PST. The Schurz NDOT sensor recorded a gust to 58 mph at 1634PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm slammed into the region on the 27th and 28th bringing extensive wind damage to the Reno-Carson City area along with a moderate snowfall to the Carson Range east of Lake Tahoe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust was recorded at the Silver Springs AWOS (along highway 50) at 1235PST. Other high winds gusts include the Lovelock ASOS with 66 mph at 1236PST and the Fallon ASOS with 64 mph at 1341PST. The high winds caused some damage (power lines and trees down and some structural damage) near Fallon. Finally, visibilities of 1/4 mile or less in blowing dust were reported in Lovelock and Fallon by the Nevada Highway Patrol.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system passing through the southern Great Basin brought two rounds of downslope winds to the eastern Sierra slopes and the Owens Valley, as well as locally high winds in southern Nevada and southeast California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three stations reported high winds - 68 mph 1 mile ESE of the Red Rock Canyon Visitors' Center, 61 mph at Nellis AFB, and 61 mph at the Henderson Executive Airport.  Broadcast media reported significant damage to the Henderson Pavilion in the Green Valley section of Henderson.  A spotter in Green Valley also reported tree limbs blown down, and a large wooden walking deck blown over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system passing through the southern Great Basin brought two rounds of downslope winds to the eastern Sierra slopes and the Owens Valley, as well as locally high winds in southern Nevada and southeast California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 6 miles SSE of Hayford Peak.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system passing through the southern Great Basin brought two rounds of downslope winds to the eastern Sierra slopes and the Owens Valley, as well as locally high winds in southern Nevada and southeast California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 12 miles SW of Elgin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific low pressure system passing through the southern Great Basin brought two rounds of downslope winds to the eastern Sierra slopes and the Owens Valley, as well as locally high winds in southern Nevada and southeast California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds ripped the roof off a patio in the northeast part of Mesquite.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front produced high winds across northern and central Nevada. Numerous wind gusts over 60 mph were observed. The high winds produced widespread blowing dust across northern Nevada reducing visibility to 1/4 mile or less at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts between 64 and 76 mph across Humboldt county with a peak wind gust of 76 mph at the Texas Springs RAWS. A semi-trailer rolled over on Interstate 80 near Golconda due to the high winds. The driver and his passenger sustained moderate injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This period of heavy snow was the second in less than 3 days.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall for this event was mostly above 7000 feet. Mt Rose Ski Area received 18 inches of snowfall while Diamond Peak received 16 inches. Meanwhile, Dagget Pass (7300 ft) only recorded 8 inches and Glenbrook (6350 ft) only saw 2 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds affected the immediate lee of the Sierra for wind-prone locations and the higher elevations around the Reno, Carson City, and Minden area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Most areas recorded wind gusts of 30-45 mph on the 12th. However, wind-prone and higher elevation sites recorded a brief period of high wind gusts on the morning of the 12th. ||The Galena RAWS (wind-prone) had wind gusts of 68-79 mph from 0400PST to 0700PST. Other higher elevation and wind-prone wind gusts include: Desert Springs RAWS with 58 mph at 0900PST, Washoe Valley with 58-63 mph from 0340PST to 0410PST, Wolf Run Golf Course with 58 mph at 0540PST, and Five Mile Flat NV DOT with 70 at 0325PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought isolated high north winds to south central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Cloud Nine balloon attraction at the south end of the Las Vegas Strip was heavily damaged, and siding was torn off a carport near the intersection of Vegas and Lamb.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Rapid snow melt caused flooding of Lamoille Creek and a mudslide in Lamoille Canyon.  Multiple residential properties and a few businesses were flooded in the town of Lamoille . Water flowed over|Lamoille highway for a brief time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rapid snow melt caused a mudslide in Lamoille Canyon about a mile upstream from the Thomas Canyon campground.  Mud and debris covered the road to a depth of 5 to 6 feet and was about 100 feet wide.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Rapid snow melt caused flooding of Lamoille Creek and a mudslide in Lamoille Canyon.  Multiple residential properties and a few businesses were flooded in the town of Lamoille . Water flowed over|Lamoille highway for a brief time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding of Lamoille Creek undermined a retaining wall in the Thomas Canyon campground.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Rapid snow melt caused flooding of Lamoille Creek and a mudslide in Lamoille Canyon.  Multiple residential properties and a few businesses were flooded in the town of Lamoille . Water flowed over|Lamoille highway for a brief time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lamoille Creek swelled by snow melt flooded multiple residential properties and a few businesses in the town of Lamoille. For a brief time water flowed over Lamoille highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wind gust to 61 mph was observed near Battle Mountain.

Southerly flow aloft around a ridge of high pressure over the Four Corners along with a disturbance moving through California brought significant moisture (around 1 inch precipitable water) for heavy rain with thunderstorms on the 16th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lyon County Sheriff Office reported shallow water over Desert Creek Road around 2200PST. However, the Desert Creek stream gauge reported much higher flow (near flood stage) between 2000PST and 2130PST so it is likely that the road was impassable in places previous to the report.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This system brought snow to the higher elevations of the Carson Range and Lake Tahoe Basin (mainly above 6000 feet).
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Diamond Peak and Mount Rose Ski Areas received 8 to 12 inches of snowfall. Observations indicate that this event was mostly rain below 6000 to 6500 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds blew down one light pole and several trees (one of which took out a power pole, knocking out power to at least 300 people), blew several shingles off at least one roof, and destroyed a carport at a condominium complex.  The three airports in the valley measured gusts of 50, 54, and 55 knots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system moving from San Francisco on the the 26th to Yuma, Arizona on the 27th caused gusty winds for western Nevada as well as widespread 0.50 to 0.75 inch rainfall totals from Carson City south to Minden and Gardnerville.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 35 to 45 mph occurred along with very isolated high wind gusts including 64 mph at the Reno National Weather Service office at 1327PST and 58 mph at the Five Mile Flat NDOT at 1325PST. Wind gusts over the higher ridges (above 8000 feet) ranged from 70-90 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hail estimated up to 1 inch in diameter was reported in Battle Mountain.
Thunderstorm winds ripped off the metal roof of a shed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds ripped off the metal roof of an open-front shed near Southside Park in Elko and carried it about 50 yards.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds flattened a green house and blew the roof off a barn.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds gusting up to 81 mph flattened a green house and blew the roof off a barn.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A potent upper low pressure system brought scattered to numerous thunderstorms with copious quantities of small hail, as well as gusty winds and locally heavy rain.
Slow moving low pressure over central and southern California combined with daytime heating to cause scattered strong thunderstorms to develop in western Nevada on the 8th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: C/O the Reno Gazette Journal:  The Nevada Highway Patrol indicated that the V and T train derailed near Gold Hill at 1730PST. An official indicated that a mudslide covered the track and caused the derailment. Twelve passengers were on the train but no one was injured. Highway 342 remained closed until around 1145PST on the 9th as the train was put back on its track.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds gusting up to 71 mph blew down a couple of trees at the Ruby View golf course and knocked down multiple large branches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds gusting to 71 mph at the Elko Airport and 60 mph at the NWS office blew down several trees at the Ruby View golf course and knocked down multiple large branches. These winds also knocked down several power lines in town which caused a couple of small fires which were quickly put out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eight inches of water and debris flowed across Aha Macav Parkway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house, setting it afire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated wind gusts to 60 mph, along with pea sized hail and very heavy rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large hail dented several cars on Interstate 15.  The hail size is an estimate, and may be underestimated considering the nature of damage reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Delayed-onset flash flooding caused by heavy rain one to two hours earlier washed out two streets and left at least two others impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot temperatures and light southerly flow aloft between a ridge of high pressure over Utah and low pressure off the California coast focused thunderstorms in the Sierra and out into extreme western Nevada.

Hot temperatures and light southerly flow aloft between a ridge of high pressure over Utah and low pressure off the California coast focused thunderstorms in the Sierra and out into extreme western Nevada.

Hot temperatures and light southerly flow aloft between a ridge of high pressure over Utah and low pressure off the California coast focused thunderstorms in the Sierra and out into extreme western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This report was from the same thunderstorm that produced the 1655PST gust at the NWS office.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds uprooted a large tree, blew over a storage shed, and toppled a sign at the library.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust to 77 mph was recorded at the Double H RAWS about 30 miles southwest of McDermitt. As the thunderstorm moved into McDermitt it blew down a large tree, overturned a storage shed, blew down a sign at the McDermitt Library, and blew off some shingles at the McDermitt Motel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds uprooted a large tree, blew over a storage shed, and toppled a sign at the library.
Sustained winds of 40 to 45 mph along with wind gusts to 55 mph were observed in West Wendover.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sustained winds to 45 mph along with wind gusts to 55 mph were observed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow was reported across portions of northern and central Nevada. Up to 6 inches fell across Humboldt county and 6 to 9 inches in White Pine County with higher amounts in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of snow was reported in both Winnemucca and McDermitt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High wind buffeted the higher passes and along the lee sides of higher mountain ranges across much of Elko county. Wind gusts as high as 68 mph were measured.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds estimated up to 80 mph knocked down some trees in Clover Valley. A Mesonet site on Pequop Summit and at Ruby Lake recorded a wind gust to 65 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow was reported across portions of northern and central Nevada. Up to 6 inches fell across Humboldt county and 6 to 9 inches in White Pine County with higher amounts in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven to eleven inches of snow was reported by most SNOTEL sites.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow was reported across portions of northern and central Nevada. Up to 6 inches fell across Humboldt county and 6 to 9 inches in White Pine County with higher amounts in the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ely, McGill, and Ruth reported 9 inches of snow and Moorman Ranch picked up 6 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought blizzard and near blizzard conditions and heavy snow to portions of northern Nevada. U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed for about 6 hours due to blizzard conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the winter weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed due to strong winds and snow creating hazardous driving conditions due to low visibility.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought blizzard and near blizzard conditions and heavy snow to portions of northern Nevada. U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed for about 6 hours due to blizzard conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the winter weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to eight inches of snow along with winds gusting to 56 mph created whiteout conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought blizzard and near blizzard conditions and heavy snow to portions of northern Nevada. U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed for about 6 hours due to blizzard conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the winter weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate to heavy snow along with wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph created blizzard-like conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents along Interstate 80 between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the weather.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought blizzard and near blizzard conditions and heavy snow to portions of northern Nevada. U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed for about 6 hours due to blizzard conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the winter weather conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusting between 40 and 50 mph along with moderate to heavy snow created near blizzard conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought blizzard and near blizzard conditions and heavy snow to portions of northern Nevada. U.S. Highway 93 between Wells and Jackpot was closed for about 6 hours due to blizzard conditions. The Nevada Highway Patrol reported 11 vehicle accidents between Battle Mountain and Wells due to the winter weather conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought 8 to 16 inches of snow to the Ruby Mountains and 6 to 12 inches to southern Eureka and southern Lander counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer in Austin reported 12 inches of snow while 6 inches was reported in Eureka.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to northern Nevada above 6000 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: SNOTELS reported between 8 and 17 inches of new snow.  Wild Horse COOP reported 11 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure remained over central and southern CA for several days bringing periods of thunderstorms with heavy rain for western NV.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two separate heavy rain events, one on the 4th and one on the 7th, caused ponding of water to more than one foot deep at the Ideal Mobile Home Park.  Other areas of Fallon had periodic standing water and minor flooding, some due to poor drainage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought 8 to 16 inches of snow to the Ruby Mountains and 6 to 12 inches to southern Eureka and southern Lander counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Most SNOTEL sites received between 8 and 16 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Downslope winds up to 88 mph were observed along the lee slopes of the Ruby Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusts up to 88 mph were observed at the Ruby Valley RAWS and up to 57 mph at the Ruby Lake RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure pushed temperatures in the Mojave Desert over Excessive Heat Warning levels.  Six people died in the Las Vegas Valley from the 18th through the 20th from the effects of the excessive heat.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Seven people died of heat-related causes between July 24th and 30th - six in the Las Vegas Valley, and one in the Lake Mead National Recreation Area. Although July was the hottest calendar month on record in Las Vegas, temperatures were below locally defined Excessive Heat Warning criteria during the last week of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One man died of heat related causes at Telephone Cove on Lake Mead.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Seven people died of heat-related causes between July 24th and 30th - six in the Las Vegas Valley, and one in the Lake Mead National Recreation Area. Although July was the hottest calendar month on record in Las Vegas, temperatures were below locally defined Excessive Heat Warning criteria during the last week of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six people died of heat-related causes in the Las Vegas Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another very strong upper level low pressure system brought more severe thunderstorms and flash flooding to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A car and trailer were floated down a wash, and debris was washed onto Northshore Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dime to nickel size hail fell for a few minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms fueled by a tropical moisture tap pounded the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days.  Extremely heavy snow and widespread flooding resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people and a dog were rescued after getting stuck behind flooded washes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms fueled by a tropical moisture tap pounded the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days.  Extremely heavy snow and widespread flooding resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Bonnie Springs Road and Arroyo Road were closed due to flooding.
